title: Building energy performance and renovation prioritisation
summary: Thermal and emissions data help cities target building renovation for maximum energy and climate benefit.
problem: Buildings account for a large share of urban energy use and emissions, but cities struggle to identify which buildings to renovate first. Whole-stock thermal surveys are expensive and quickly outdated.
solution: CAMS residential-emissions data and CLMS land-cover products, combined with drone-based thermal imaging and building information, support digital twins that identify worst-performing buildings. Planners can prioritise renovation for the greatest energy and emissions benefit.

copernicusRole.detail: CAMS emissions and CLMS land cover are fused with drone thermal imagery, BIM and IoT data into a digital twin. Copernicus is one input among several in a multi-source workflow.
